mnemonics.title: ML Types Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: S-U-R: Supervised Uses labeled data, Unsupervised finds hidden patterns, Reinforcement gets rewards for actions.

mnemonics.title: Overfitting vs Underfitting
mnemonics.content: OVER-ENTHUSIASTIC = Overfitting (too excited about training data), UNDER-ACHIEVER = Underfitting (too lazy to learn patterns).

mnemonics.title: Evaluation Metrics
mnemonics.content: AP-F1-R: Accuracy (overall correct), Precision (positive predictions correct), F1-Score (balance of P & R), Recall (actual positives found).

acronyms.usage: Field of AI enabling systems to learn from data
Processing and understanding human language by computers
Deep learning architecture for image processing
Neural network for sequential data processing
Type of RNN for long-term dependency learning
Metric for measuring prediction error in regression

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a factory assembly line where each layer (input, hidden, output) processes and transforms information progressively, like workers passing partially assembled products.
Picture a ball rolling down a mountainous terrain (loss function) to the lowest point (optimal weights), with gravity representing the learning rate determining step size.
Think of organizing a messy closet by grouping similar items (clothes, shoes, accessories) together - that's clustering, finding natural groups in data.
Use target practice analogy: High bias = consistently missing the bullseye in one direction (underfitting), High variance = shots scattered everywhere (overfitting).

analogies.analogy: Like teaching a child to recognize animals: show many pictures (training data), let them identify patterns (learn features), and they'll recognize new animals (generalize).
Like learning with a teacher who provides answers (labels) - students (algorithm) learn to match inputs with correct outputs.
Like exploring a new city without a map - you'll discover natural neighborhoods and landmarks (clusters) by observing patterns in streets and buildings.
Like training a dog with treats: good behavior gets rewards (positive feedback), bad behavior gets corrections - the dog learns optimal behavior over time.
Like the human brain's visual cortex processing - simple edges first, then shapes, then complex objects -层层递进 (layer by layer progression).
